name: Paper- Bag Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup butter or margarine, one stick
recipeInstructions:
1. Make an unbaked 9-inch pastry shell.
2. Peel, quarter, and core the apples. Then halve each quarter crosswise to make chunks (approximately 7 cups total).
3. Place the apple chunks in a large bowl.
4. In a separate cup, combine 1/2 cup sugar, 2 tablespoons flour, and 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg. Sprinkle this mixture over the apples and toss to coat evenly.
5. Spoon the filling into the pastry shell.
6. Drizzle with lemon juice.
7. In another small bowl, mix together 1/2 cup sugar and 2 tablespoons flour for the topping. Cut in the butter or margarine; sprinkle over the apples to cover.
8. Fold a heavy brown paper bag large enough to cover the pie loosely over it. Fold the open end of the bag twice and secure with paper clips.
9. Place the assembly on a large cookie sheet for easy handling.
10. Bake in an electric oven set to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C) for 1 hour, or until apples are tender and topping is bubbly and golden.
11. Carefully open the bag; remove the pie and cool on a wire rack.
12. Serve plain or with cheese or ice cream. Yield: 1 (9-inch) pie. Be careful.
